question: Which users have the highest learning completion rates and what activities did they complete?
instructions: Identify top-performing learners by completion rate and list their completed activities to recognize high achievers and understand successful learning patterns.
sql: SELECT p.firstname, p.lastname, p.companyname, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT act.activityme ORDER BY act.activityme SEPARATOR ', ') as completed_activities FROM person_sumtotal p JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk JOIN tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act ON a.activityfk = act.activity_pk WHERE p.active = 'Y' AND a.completionstatus = 'Completed' GROUP BY p.personpk, p.firstname, p.lastname, p.companyname HAVING COUNT(a.attempt_pk) >= 3 ORDER BY completion_rate DESC, total_attempts DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Identify top learners and their achievements. 2. Join person, attempt, and activity tables. 3. Calculate completion rate as percentage. 4. Filter for active users with multiple attempts. 5. Group by person and aggregate activities. 6. Sort by completion rate and total attempts.

question: What are the most challenging learning activities based on low completion rates?
instructions: Find activities with low completion rates to identify content that may need review or redesign for better learner success.
sql: SELECT act.activityme as activity_name, act.code as activity_code, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityme, act.code HAVING COUNT(a.attempt_pk) >= 10 ORDER BY completion_rate ASC, avg_score ASC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Identify challenging courses needing improvement. 2. Join activity and attempt tables. 3. Calculate completion rate percentage. 4. Filter for active activities with sufficient data. 5. Sort by lowest completion rate first. 6. Include average score for additional context.

question: Which organizations have the highest learning engagement and completion rates?
instructions: Compare organizational learning performance to identify best practices and areas for improvement across business units.
sql: SELECT o.name as organization_name, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as total_users, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(a.attempt_pk) / COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk), 2) as avg_attempts_per_user, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM organization_sumtotal o JOIN personorganization_sumtotal po ON o.organizationpk = po.organizationfk JOIN person_sumtotal p ON po.personfk = p.personpk LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk WHERE o.deleted = 'N' AND p.active = 'Y' GROUP BY o.organizationpk, o.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) >= 10 ORDER BY completion_rate DESC, avg_attempts_per_user DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Compare organizational learning performance. 2. Join organization, person-organization, and person tables. 3. Calculate metrics per organization. 4. Filter for active organizations and users. 5. Include only organizations with sufficient users. 6. Sort by completion rate and engagement.

question: How many users are currently enrolled in learning activities and what is their progress status?
instructions: Monitor current learning status to track active learners and their progress across the platform for real-time engagement monitoring.
sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as enrolled_users, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'In Progress' THEN 1 END) as in_progress_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Not Started' THEN 1 END) as not_started_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a WHERE a.currentattemptind = 'Y'
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Monitor current learning activity. 2. Focus on current attempts only. 3. Count users and attempts by status. 4. Calculate overall completion rate. 5. Provide comprehensive status overview. 6. No grouping needed for global metrics.

question: What is the average time spent on learning activities by activity type?
instructions: Analyze learning duration patterns to optimize course design and understand learner time investment across different activities.
sql: SELECT act.activityme as activity_name, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.elapsedseconds AS DECIMAL) / 60), 2) as avg_minutes_spent, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.elapsedseconds AS DECIMAL) / 3600), 2) as avg_hours_spent, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE a.elapsedseconds IS NOT NULL AND a.elapsedseconds > 0 GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityme ORDER BY avg_hours_spent DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Understand time investment per activity type. 2. Join activity and attempt tables. 3. Convert elapsed seconds to minutes and hours. 4. Filter out null or zero duration records. 5. Group by activity and calculate averages. 6. Sort by highest time investment.

question: Which users have not completed any learning activities in the last 90 days?
instructions: Identify inactive learners requiring re-engagement strategies and follow-up to improve platform adoption and user retention.
sql: SELECT p.firstname, p.lastname, p.companyname, p.createddate, MAX(a.enddt) as last_activity_date, DATEDIFF(CURRENT_DATE, MAX(a.enddt)) as days_since_last_activity FROM person_sumtotal p LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk WHERE p.active = 'Y' AND p.deleted = 'N' GROUP BY p.personpk, p.firstname, p.lastname, p.companyname, p.createddate HAVING MAX(a.enddt) IS NULL OR DATEDIFF(CURRENT_DATE, MAX(a.enddt)) > 90 ORDER BY days_since_last_activity DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Identify inactive learners for re-engagement. 2. Left join to include users with no attempts. 3. Filter for active, non-deleted users. 4. Calculate days since last activity. 5. Include users with no activity or >90 days inactive. 6. Sort by longest inactive period.

question: What is the distribution of learning activities by activity label and their performance?
instructions: Analyze learning performance by activity labels to understand category effectiveness and optimize curriculum structure.
sql: SELECT al.labelname as activity_label, COUNT(act.activity_pk) as total_activities, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_learners, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score FROM actlabel_sumtotal al JOIN tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act ON al.actlabelpk = act.activitylabelfk LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' GROUP BY al.actlabelpk, al.labelname ORDER BY total_attempts DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Analyze performance by activity categories. 2. Join activity labels, activities, and attempts. 3. Calculate completion rates and average scores. 4. Filter for active activities only. 5. Group by activity label. 6. Sort by total attempts for popularity.

question: What are the peak learning hours and days based on activity start times?
instructions: Analyze learning time patterns to optimize course scheduling and identify peak learning periods for resource planning.
sql: SELECT HOUR(a.startdt) as learning_hour, DAYNAME(a.startdt) as learning_day, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_learners, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a WHERE a.startdt >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 30 DAY) GROUP BY HOUR(a.startdt), DAYNAME(a.startdt) ORDER BY total_attempts DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Identify optimal learning times. 2. Extract hour and day from start timestamps. 3. Focus on recent activity (last 30 days). 4. Count attempts and unique learners. 5. Calculate completion rates by time slot. 6. Sort by activity volume.

question: Which learning activities have the highest launch counts and what does this indicate about popularity?
instructions: Analyze course popularity based on launch frequency to prioritize content development and resource allocation.
sql: SELECT act.activityme as activity_name, act.code as activity_code, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.launchcount AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_launch_count, ROUND(MAX(CAST(a.launchcount AS DECIMAL)), 2) as max_launch_count, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_learners FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' AND a.launchcount IS NOT NULL GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityme, act.code ORDER BY avg_launch_count DESC, total_attempts DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Identify most popular courses by launch frequency. 2. Join activities and attempts tables. 3. Calculate average and maximum launch counts. 4. Filter for active activities with launch data. 5. Group by activity and aggregate metrics. 6. Sort by average launch count.

question: What is the learning progress distribution by user demographics (gender, company)?
instructions: Analyze learning performance by demographics to ensure equitable learning opportunities and identify potential barriers.
sql: SELECT p.gendercode, p.companyname, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as total_users, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score FROM person_sumtotal p LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk WHERE p.active = 'Y' AND p.deleted = 'N' GROUP BY p.gendercode, p.companyname HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) >= 5 ORDER BY completion_rate DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Ensure equitable learning opportunities. 2. Group by gender and company demographics. 3. Calculate completion rates and scores by group. 4. Filter for active, non-deleted users. 5. Include only groups with sufficient users. 6. Sort by completion rate for performance comparison.

question: Which organizations have the most diverse learning activity participation?
instructions: Analyze organizational learning diversity to identify comprehensive learning cultures and knowledge sharing patterns.
sql: SELECT o.name as organization_name, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as total_users, COUNT(DISTINCT a.activityfk) as unique_activities, COUNT(DISTINCT act.activityme) as unique_activity_names, ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT a.activityfk) / COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk), 2) as activities_per_user FROM organization_sumtotal o JOIN personorganization_sumtotal po ON o.organizationpk = po.organizationfk JOIN person_sumtotal p ON po.personfk = p.personpk LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act ON a.activityfk = act.activity_pk WHERE o.deleted = 'N' AND p.active = 'Y' GROUP BY o.organizationpk, o.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) >= 10 ORDER BY unique_activities DESC, activities_per_user DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Identify organizations with diverse learning cultures. 2. Join organization, person, and activity tables. 3. Count unique activities and activity names. 4. Calculate activities per user ratio. 5. Filter for active organizations with sufficient users. 6. Sort by activity diversity metrics.

question: What is the correlation between learning duration and success rates across different activities?
instructions: Analyze the relationship between learning duration and success rates to optimize course design and time allocation.
sql: SELECT act.activityme as activity_name, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.elapsedseconds AS DECIMAL) / 60), 2) as avg_minutes, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.success = 'true' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as success_rate, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE a.elapsedseconds IS NOT NULL AND a.elapsedseconds > 0 GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityme HAVING COUNT(a.attempt_pk) >= 10 ORDER BY success_rate DESC, avg_minutes ASC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Understand time investment vs success relationship. 2. Join activities and attempts tables. 3. Convert elapsed seconds to minutes. 4. Calculate success rate percentage. 5. Filter for activities with sufficient data. 6. Sort by success rate and time efficiency.

question: How many users are currently enrolled in learning activities and what is their progress status?
instructions: Monitor current learning status to track active learners and their progress across the platform for real-time engagement monitoring.
sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as enrolled_users, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'In Progress' THEN 1 END) as in_progress_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Not Started' THEN 1 END) as not_started_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a WHERE a.currentattemptind = 'Y'
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Monitor current learning activity. 2. Focus on current attempts only. 3. Count users and attempts by status. 4. Calculate overall completion rate. 5. Provide comprehensive status overview. 6. No grouping needed for global metrics.

question: What is the average number of attempts per user for each learning activity?
instructions: Analyze attempt patterns to understand course difficulty and learner persistence across different activities.
sql: SELECT act.activityme as activity_name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_learners, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(a.attempt_pk) / COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk), 2) as avg_attempts_per_learner, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.currentattemptind = 'Y' THEN 1 END) as current_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityme ORDER BY avg_attempts_per_learner DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Understand retry patterns and course difficulty. 2. Join activities and attempts tables. 3. Calculate attempts per learner ratio. 4. Include current attempts and completion rates. 5. Filter for active activities only. 6. Sort by highest retry rates.

question: Which learning activities require registration and how does this affect participation?
instructions: Analyze the impact of registration requirements on learning participation to optimize course accessibility.
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN act.openforreg = 'Y' THEN 'Registration Required' ELSE 'No Registration Required' END as registration_status, COUNT(act.activity_pk) as total_activities, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_participants, ROUND(COUNT(a.attempt_pk) / COUNT(act.activity_pk), 2) as avg_attempts_per_activity, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' GROUP BY act.openforreg ORDER BY avg_attempts_per_activity DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Compare participation between registration types. 2. Categorize activities by registration requirement. 3. Calculate participation metrics by category. 4. Filter for active activities only. 5. Group by registration status. 6. Sort by participation levels.

question: What is the learning activity participation pattern for users by their start date?
instructions: Analyze learning participation by user tenure to understand onboarding effectiveness and long-term engagement patterns.
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 30 DAY) THEN 'New (30 days)' WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 90 DAY) THEN 'Recent (90 days)' WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 365 DAY) THEN 'Mature (1 year)' ELSE 'Established (1+ years)' END as user_tenure, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as total_users, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(a.attempt_pk) / COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk), 2) as avg_attempts_per_user, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM person_sumtotal p LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk WHERE p.active = 'Y' AND p.deleted = 'N' GROUP BY CASE WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 30 DAY) THEN 'New (30 days)' WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 90 DAY) THEN 'Recent (90 days)' WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 365 DAY) THEN 'Mature (1 year)' ELSE 'Established (1+ years)' END ORDER BY total_users DESC
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Understand engagement by user tenure. 2. Categorize users by start date ranges. 3. Calculate participation metrics by tenure. 4. Filter for active, non-deleted users. 5. Group by tenure categories. 6. Sort by user count for popularity.

question: Create a comprehensive learning dashboard showing key metrics across all SumTotal Learn data.
instructions: Calculate comprehensive learning platform metrics across all models for executive-level platform assessment and reporting.
sql: SELECT 'Total Users' as metric, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as value FROM person_sumtotal p WHERE p.active = 'Y' AND p.deleted = 'N' UNION ALL SELECT 'Active Organizations', COUNT(DISTINCT o.organizationpk) FROM organization_sumtotal o WHERE o.deleted = 'N' UNION ALL SELECT 'Learning Activities', COUNT(DISTINCT act.activity_pk) FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act WHERE act.active = 'Y' UNION ALL SELECT 'Total Learning Attempts', COUNT(a.attempt_pk) FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a UNION ALL SELECT 'Completed Attempts', COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a UNION ALL SELECT 'Successful Attempts', COUNT(CASE WHEN a.success = 'true' THEN 1 END) FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a UNION ALL SELECT 'Average Score', ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a WHERE a.score IS NOT NULL
chain_of_thought: 1. Goal: Provide executive-level platform overview. 2. Use UNION ALL to combine different metrics. 3. Calculate key platform statistics. 4. Filter for active, relevant records. 5. Include both counts and averages. 6. Present as key-value pairs for dashboard display.
